LW_PlayerLog_AI_Containment_Constructed.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "E271232B45F1204B932078BC94E59935", "I have no idea what just happened.

Roland gave me plans he downloaded from Kappa for an AI Containment facility suitable for Stanley, which I promptly built and got powered up, slotted his core into the cradle, and then…

He was back! For a minute. He started rambling on about sentience, which is its own barrel of monkeys that I don’t want to even think about right now, but he seemed… hopeful. Like he’d escaped everything down there.

Out of nowhere, some sort of ‘sideband signal’ (code minion nonsense) comes through and he just stops talking!

I scanned his containment facility on Roland’s recommendation, and suddenly our comms channel is hit with this garbled gobbledegook, and someone spoke to me! Someone that WASN’T from ARC!

I have no idea how they did it, but after rebuilding our comms tower (again, Roland’s very urgent recommendation…), it happened AGAIN!

Some sleazeball, hacking into our comms, and they did something to Stanley! Disabled him, shut him down… I’m not sure.

He said I need to check out a lockbox inside the quarters of Lisa Chapman in the Epsilon facility… She was the chief engineer, the one we found in the Mu facility, along with her keycard. He insinuated that he’d restore Stanley if we took a look…

I need that Kappa Module info from Stanley, so I’m not sure I have much choice. If whatever’s in this lockbox doesn’t provide some serious answers though, it might be time to give Trudy a ring and get this whole thing sorted out.

It could all be getting too complicated at this point.")

LW_PlayerLog_AfterIota.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "CA39B5484FC485633B979DA48D35AE69", "We found the Iota facility! And… Grandad.

Well, obviously not Grandad himself. But a mention of him! He was there, in Iota! Dr. Gerard Shaw... He was some sort of medical doctor here?!

I knew there was more going on with this claim than he had let on, but I didn’t realise he was actually HERE… I wish he could have told me before he passed, instead of all those cryptic hints and references…

Well, it’s too late for that now, but if he was here, it’s confirmation that we’re on the right track! I’m convinced that this Resistance guy knows more than he’s letting on, so it’s time to put my foot down a bit and get some answers.

Side note:
It seems like Iota connects to the manufacturing process back at Tau in some way - something to do with the weird ore we found here, used in the accelerator, I think? Might be worth heading back there and trying to trigger a batch of fuel rods at some point.")

LW_PlayerLog_AfterKryptos.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1CD2ED6244242099AD69CE8D2472FA6E", "Grandad said there was ‘no light in Kryptos’ but I didn’t realise how right he was… What a horrible place.

I can’t believe that the gentle, kind man who helped raise me could have done the things he did there… But the logs don’t lie, do they? He even admitted it in these ‘stub memories’…

How do I reconcile that behaviour with the man I thought he was?

My head’s spinning. The things they did in Kryptos are horrific… I’ve found several log files featuring ‘Caleb Vihaan’ before, but never imagined that I’d read about him being lured to a horrible underground facility, and having his brain surgically removed, his consciousness merged with an AI construct…

Grandad… why? All this… evil, just to try and extract this ‘Element A’? How can any resource be worth this…")

LW_PlayerLog_AfterOmicron.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "080FD5BE448C64FCCF663DB702464EA9", "Oh, Grandad… What the hell were you involved in, here? Another message, and more new questions than answers… 

Turns out that Grandad was Head of AI Research for this Virtus expedition. A real bigwig. 

They were working on something that would let them extract something called ‘Element A’, safely. It was so dangerous that they couldn’t do it by hand, apparently…

There are more answers to be found in a place called ‘Kryptos', though from what Grandad was saying, it’s not somewhere I’m super keen to visit…

I have to know what was going on here, and how Grandad was involved, so we’re just going to have to keep going and find this place. It seems like it’s so well hidden that the only way to get there is via another facility - Sigma - which requires the use of some sort of anti-gravity-tether ‘bridge' over the magma lake.

Problem is, the ‘Tear Paper’ lockdown we’ve encountered across all these facilities has made the bridge inoperable. Roland and I think that if anyone can get the bridge working again, it’s our weird old friend Stanley, so we’ve got to find a way to reactivate him.

As an aside, Omicron is the final station for the Scarpa Climber - the giant elevator in the crater facility borehole - and until now we’ve been unable to get here via that route. Turns out that someone had sabotaged the safety systems, which explains why it wasn’t able to take us there… I think it might even have been Lisa Chapman, that project engineer who we thought we’d found back in the Mu facility.

Turns out it wasn’t her, and she’s actually still there in Omicron. She wasn’t quite right at the end, I think… Roland thinks she was being blackmailed. Poor woman.

Anyway, I’m confident that I can get the climber working again if we restore the machinery in the maintenance section below the climber chamber. By doing that, we’d have a quick route straight back to the Outpost which we can use to bring up any cargo we need to! Much easier than lugging it all through the teleporter myself…")

LW_PlayerLog_AfterSigma.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "FF31C9554D95897959C9DF8F47FCE4D7", "I can’t believe it. Sigma isn’t just the key to locating Kryptos, but a treasure trove of information on ARC’s dirty dealings…

It seems to have been some sort of surveillance system - it calls itself ‘Middleman’ - responsible for intercepting ARC communications, analyzing them, and creating reports for Virtus Ltd.

First off, cheerfully, the system gave me some choice interceptions of conversations between Trudy and I since we arrived at the claim… Turns out she WAS set up as my rep on purpose - it wasn’t chance at all. As well as that, she’s very close to figuring out what’s going on with this place, or at least that something weird happened here and all is not as it seems...

Some of the data on the system in Sigma is absolutely crazy - I grew up hearing tales about the ‘Kirkby Rebellion’ - the heroic actions of ARC and the evil sentient AI monsters… Every child knows about it!
To find out that it could actually have all been staged by ARC is just… unthinkable. But, then again... it’s ARC - the older I get, the more I realise that their 'truth' does not always match reality.

ARC obviously have a keen interest in keeping all the information in this facility from going public, so I’ll be passing it on to the Resistance. I can’t help but think it unlikely, but perhaps they’ll face some sort of repercussions for everything they’ve done…

Anyway, it’s time to face the music and head to Kryptos. Really hope it’s not as bad as I feel like it’s going to be.")

LW_PlayerLog_AfterTau.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "F897504B4714EB6FFB182E86EF0213F7", "The facility beyond Rho is called Tau, and it was filled to the brim with this radioactive, toxic goop that had started to spew out into the cavern…

I managed to reset and flush the waste system in the facility, which appears to be a manufacturing center. This expedition was no small thing, the extent of these different facilities is vast - a serious industrial undertaking! For what exact purpose however, I am yet to discover…

When we left Tau, Roland experienced some sort of… code-minion-thing, involving his… stubs? Something related to ‘Memories’. Anyway, it connects to the Kappa Module, and something called ‘Iota’ - it must be another facility.

These huge pipes coming into Tau must come from somewhere, so we’ll follow them and find it - maybe they’ll lead us to Iota!")

LW_PlayerLog_BeforeOmicron.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "7D7D7CE84F435B98E941AAB9920A3F3F", "Seam X-5 really is the motherlode! HYPERION CRYSTAL! Grandad brought us to the richest vein of Hyperion Crystal that I’ve ever heard of, and this stuff is so incredibly rare…

Grandad left us another message in the cavern, inside an old habitat, along with his old expedition keycard… Turns out he really was high up in this venture - Chief Scientist, no less. He always told me he was an engineer…

Well, the message he left was directly for us, this time - I have to say I got a little emotional hearing him talk to me... I didn’t think I’d ever get that again.

He was his usual mysterious self, and implied that he’d done bad things here… He told us to find a facility called Omicron, and a room for secure records stored there. I don’t know if I really want to find out what he’s talking about, but I owe it to him after bringing us to find such riches.

Oh, and while we look for Omicron, I should find a way to get across to that Hyperion Crystal - it’s incredibly hot and dangerous in that cavern, so I should set up a safer network of power relays and oxygen generators using the power piped in from the outside facilities. 

The Black Market I constructed seems to have off-the-books gravity tether upgrades available, so I’ll look into that and maybe Roland can help me get across those big, ominous, lava (magma) filled gaps!")

LW_PlayerLog_BeforeSigma.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "420F4F704EC446E1CAE97FA99EF546B7", "We made it to Sigma, but there’s an issue with the main control room underneath the giant communications dish. Seems like there’s a dangerous buildup of electromagnetism in there which would kill Roland in an instant. As much as I sometimes wish he’d keep his opinions to himself, I don’t want him erased, so it looks like we’ve got to solve that issue before we can find the location of Kryptos.

According to the man (drone) himself, it’s a simple case of rejigging a bunch of repeater towers across this giant cavern - at Rho, Tau and Omicron - and we’ll be able to use our huge (somehow covert…) comms tower back in the Outpost to resolve the problem.

At least we’ve got the use of these amazing teleporters to save me the legwork of running back and forth to these repeaters. Thanks Lisa Chapman, sorry again about the whole… blackmail, sabotage, death… thing.")

LW_PlayerLog_BeforeTau.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "DE2D8828422D212F1A41B9858EEF97D2", "After uploading the data drive we found to the Resistance, Roland tried to use the decompiler that they provided to unlock the Kappa Module, even with Stanley in his suspended state.

No luck, unfortunately, but he’s sure that finding more data is the key to unlocking it - that means exploring beyond Rho and finding more Gaia data servers. There seems to be another facility beyond Rho, so that’s where I’m aiming for next.

These facilities are located in a vast underground cavern surrounding a huge magma lake… I thought that the underground jungle might have been the peak of the wonder of this place, but this takes the biscuit.

The cavern is incredibly hot, overwhelming my suit’s cooling systems if I stay out there too long - the atmosphere generators set up in this area also have cooling capability if I can provide them with industrial coolant, so I should do that and make sure I hop between them to keep cool.

I also discovered something neat - portable construction packages, called ‘Buildkits’. It seems like I can use them to build new infrastructure within this region - atmo-generators, power relays, that sort of thing. Should allow me to create my own network of safe paths through the area, very useful!")

LW_PlayerLog_BeforeX5.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "9319BD00480F2B767B7E6C9309823445", "After Iota, I confronted the ‘Resistance’ figure about what was going on - turns out they did know more than they were letting on.

He told us that Virtus Ltd - a competitor to ARC and the second-most powerful megacorp in existence - is behind the facilities we’ve been finding. They were apparently looking for some sort of fantastical new element… A dangerous one, judging from the log files we found in Iota.

We built a new comms tower at the Outpost, with plans sent over by the Resistance, which is supposed to be ‘covert’ and got us access to a new code-decompiler… ‘Covert’ apparently means fifty-feet-high and hugely demanding on our power grid, but Roland seems to think it’s stealthy enough…

Anyway, main thing is that with a little crafting and some ‘genius AI drone’ know-how, I whipped us up a device that let Roland interface with the Kappa Module through Stanley’s housing.

The Kappa Module is a treasure map! Some sort of convoluted, just-for-us, message-from-dead-Grandad treasure map, unlocked by Roland’s ‘stubs’!

I actually heard Grandad’s voice again… He was recording some sort of survey report, but it’s absolutely just a cover for a message that only Roland and I would understand!

He’s pointed us to some sort of cavern called ‘Seam X-5’ - it’s apparently filled with 34 million credits' worth of minerals… If it pans out, that’s the future of the Outpost sorted! We’re going to go and check it out, maybe there’s more info from Grandad down there!")

LW_PlayerLog_Crystal.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "196BBC6E49B3C2A4EF38F1B15ACC8B56", "Our wee pal Stanley is back, and he managed to reactivate the tether bridge across to Sigma! I left him to pick through our archives at the Outpost, must be a lot to catch up on after all that time trapped underground…

The tether gondola took us across to a mine site - ‘Hades' - where I had one of the creepiest experiences of my life. A giant, floating crystal… bewitched me? That sounds strange, but that’s the only way I can really describe it. I heard whispers, then a voice in my head. Booming, overwhelming. All I could do was get closer to the crystal. It was… blinding.

Roland and the medical drone from the mine site managed to drag me away after I passed out. Lucky for me I keep that little guy around, he’s a handy protector when push comes to shove!

The crystal is blocking the only way forward to Sigma, so we’ve got to get rid of it - there are a bunch of industrial mining lasers in the area, which we think we can reprogram to focus their beams directly on the crystal. Hopefully the combined power of all of them will blow that thing apart and let us get past without any other mental… complications.")

LW_PlayerLog_Epsilon_FacilityExposition.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "69AE7A644B40582C022422B991154529", "I found the Kappa facility... What I didn’t expect was that it's actually underwater.

It seems like there was some complex system of dams and water control going on - I have no idea why these people would want to construct a facility literally inside a lake, but they did!

I managed to find my way into the dam, but the control room is sealed behind three locked doors - each opens only with a personal keycard belonging to a supervisor from the local facilities. Once I find all three, I can drain the water from the lake and get into Kappa.

There’s a keycard pinging in Epsilon, where the climber is, but I’ve not found it yet. Worth checking, might be the easiest one to find!

There’s still no sign of where this Stanley guy is, and he doesn’t seem to be in too much of a hurry to be rescued… He’s kind of creeping me out, if I’m honest.

Anyway, I can choose which of the local facilities to explore first - Roland gave me the rundown on each:

Theta:
A bioengineering and hydroponics research facility... There might be useful plans and blueprints there related to growing plants, or for creating useful items that can help me explore.

Mu:
This is where they generate their power, apparently. I'm sure I can jury-rig some of their tech for use back at the Outpost.

Lambda:
No supervisor there, but it's an archaeology research facility, which is just... pretty cool. Bound to be some valuable stuff there, maybe even new tech?")

LW_PlayerLog_Epsilon_Intro.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "ADB4EAD846714C051059E48F27C18F4F", "I've stepped straight into some sort of fairytale... A vast underground jungle, deep within the planet.

Somehow, despite the lack of natural light down here, the plant life is overabundant! It's overgrown everything, the man-made structures are gradually returning to nature. There’s something poetic about that.

Anyway, this strange new character we heard on the comms channel is called ‘Stanley'. It’s been hard to get anything much more than his name out of him, I think he must have lost it after years trapped down here? How he’s managed to survive so long is beyond me, but if he’s alive, then there must be others too.

I’ve reset the external power for the ‘Epsilon’ facility we found ourselves in, so we can go out and explore this amazing place. Roland says that this Stanley-character’s carrier wave has modulated, whatever that means, and he’s used it to grab the co-ordinates for a bunch of other facilities in the area, including… Kappa!

Time to go and find this 'Module' and see what Grandad wanted me to discover!")

LW_PlayerLog_FinalStub.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "87D5AA704BE2795071A0908A62F7C35C", "I’m so tired… Tired of everything I do being foiled by things outside of my control. Tired of feeling like EVERYTHING is rigged against me.

The ‘Resistance’? They were really Virtus Ltd, the whole time... Stringing me along like a puppet, getting me to deliver them the data they’d lost when they lost contact with the expedition.

Is it even possible to succeed here? How do I win, if the game is rigged from the start?… The Hyperion Crystal on my claim would mean I’d never need to work again, but what’s to stop ARC just coming in and taking it through some loophole? If they did, what could I even do to stop them?…

Speaking of ARC, worse than everything we’ve discovered, than everything Grandad did… Is that ARC have been doing it for YEARS, and are STILL doing it.

According to him, what Virtus - his team - were doing here was just attempting to reverse-engineer ARC’s work - classic corporate espionage.

It’s all in pursuit of this incredible material - ‘Aetherium’, ARC call it. It’s the key to their anti-gravity tech, and the reason for their dominance over all the other megacorps.

The info we found in Sigma, about Kirkby - the Rebellion, the ban on sentient AI. It’s all because of this one element. ARC locked down all development of sentient AI under the guise of protecting everyone, when in reality they themselves continued in secret, and have used it ever since to extract Aetherium.

I only have one choice - we have to blow this whole thing wide open. People need to KNOW what’s going on.

We’re going to need to prepare…")

LW_PlayerLog_Kappa_DamTime.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "970DBA784EA370F637B318859E0A6912", "That’s all the keycards collected for the doors down in the dam!

Time to head into that control room, get the lake drained, then find this Kappa Module.

Still haven’t found Stanley. He’s helped, in his own (weird) way. I’d like to help him too, if I can.

Time to find whatever it is that Grandad intended for me here.")

LW_PlayerLog_Kappa_StanleyFound.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "7822F34A4AA2DE708A7376BC863417B3", "I can’t believe it. He’s a bloody AI construct.

Stanley, that is. Bumbling old, kinda-cute-in-a-weird-way, actually-an-AI STANLEY!

How could we miss the signs? Ugh. Stupid, stupid!

I guess it explains how he’s ‘survived’ all this time, when everyone else was dead…

He says he’s not the Kappa Module that Grandad wanted me to find, but that he contains that data within him!

I need to find a new home for him in the Outpost so that he can tell me what it is we’ve trekked all this way for, paid so many credits for, spent so much TIME for!

It better be bloody good.")

LW_PlayerLog_Lambda_Discovered.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1F2357BD42277D6BD4494B886E7049D0", "I checked out the Lambda facility - it sits right in the middle of a huge swamp filled with giant skeletons… Spooky.

The facility also seems to be in the middle of a nuclear meltdown, which is pretty classic 'Maeve luck'.

The Lambda reactor is giving off a ton of radiation, and Roland is convinced it’ll destroy this whole place if we don’t fix it, so I guess I’m going to be adding ‘nuclear engineer’ to my resumé after this little adventure.

Fortunately, there’s a remote-access system for the reactor. I just need to gather the components for a new reactor core, put it into that system and it’ll do the replacement for me - no need to enter the reactor room and give myself a third arm, or something.

I need to gather both:

- A Fuel Rod Housing: I can craft one at the Electronics Workbench.
- A Fuel Rod: We think there’ll be one in the Secure Storage cage at the Epsilon facility.

Once I’ve got these, I can combine them into a Reactor Core at the Electronics Workbench.

Then it’s just a matter of bringing it back to Lambda, slotting it into the A.R.M.S and hey presto, meltdown averted!")

LW_PlayerLog_Lambda_Fixed.JournalEntryHeader_27_A9E4541846447E9F29: NSLOCTEXT("", "5F455E904E717CDA066C5DA02B9220A9", "Hero of the Spooky Underground Jungle")
LW_PlayerLog_Lambda_Fixed.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "678C5A994452E0B2DB7F769D44687FD0", "I did it! Meltdown averted, Lambda and this weird jungle are safe!

There should be some cool stuff to find in this place, and now I can look around properly.

Seems like they were digging up and analyzing all these skeletons as well as geological samples from the surrounding swamps.

Bound to be some valuable tech here to find. There’s a bunker here too actually, like the other facilities, though no keycard ping. Still worth checking out though!")

LW_PlayerLog_Mu_Discovered.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "67A3A9A048316984D7EAC280315385C8", "The Mu facility is crazy… Huge, overgrown plants swarm the whole thing and have even infested the interior spaces, blocking the way deeper into the habitats.

There are also massive glowing plants all around this area, and a bunch of solar panels pointed right at them… It seems like they were somehow obtaining energy from the light they give off... What an ingenious method of power generation for this strange place!

Maybe I can find some way to repurpose these panels to generate power for the Outpost…

Anyway. I’m going to have to find some way to remove these plants to get further into Mu - Roland’s ‘laser fangs’ aren’t going to help, they’re tuned for dirt and rock, not plant life…

There’ll be a way forward, there always is, and one of the Kappa keycards is pinging at the bunker deeper into the facility, so we have to get through and find it!")

LW_PlayerLog_Mu_Explored.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "C92688B943953F29435694A2AF36DD9D", "We made it through the overgrown plants at Mu and found the bunker. There’s more foliage here blocking the bunker itself from opening, but I am confident that the herbicide I whipped up and have been using to great effect so far will get us in.

Another keycard awaiting inside this bunker, and we’re another step closer to getting through those triple-locked doors in the dam.

There’s more to explore further into the facility, maybe some useful blueprints? Main priority is the keycard, though.

Grandad, all this work better be worth it…")

LW_PlayerLog_Omega.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "793049BD4716568FDE9E049105CB428C", "This is it. My final log entry before we head to Omega.

I was looking back at my very first entry, just after we arrived - ‘Underwhelming’ was the word I used. If only I’d known what we were in for…

Subterranean facilities, underground jungles, lava caverns, unethical experiments… This place has it all. 

ARC only knows what the Omega facility holds for Roland, Stanley and I… But I’m certain that together we’ll overcome it.

It’s time to shine a very bright light on the very dark secrets of this claim. Time to expose the true cost of Aetherium, ARC’S anti-grav tech, their AI research, the destruction of Kirkby International…. all of it. Virtus have a lot to answer for, too - they’re not by any means innocent in this whole thing.

Once I start that call with Trudy, there’s no going back. The Kuiper Protocol will hit in just a few minutes, so I’m going to have to be quick.

It’s time.")

LW_PlayerLog_Resistance.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "21C769E9449DEA2647CFCBA12582A7E4", "Chapman’s lockbox contained an air-gapped, encrypted data drive. The sort that the corpos use to move around data they don’t want to send over a network…

We brought it back to the Outpost, where the sleazeball comms-hacker introduced himself as a member of some sort of anti-ARC resistance…

I don’t want to get involved in any of that - I just want Stanley back. That said, this guy told me he’s willing to pay well for more drives like the one we found, and even provide special upgrades. They could really help us explore this claim further, dig deeper, find more valuable resources…

Apparently, Lisa Chapman was working on small-scale teleporter tech - I’ve never had the chance to use anything like that, workers on the ground don’t get that luxury… But now I have blueprints for one, and the weird tech that makes it work. I’ve got to try it out, right?…")

LW_PlayerLog_Rho.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "39528F7D4C5B5EE3C8D0EE9FBD734175", "That was a wee bit scary!

The teleporter took me to a new facility - ‘Rho’. Only issue… Roland didn’t come with me!

It was really strange to not have him chirping away while I explored the place - a power generation facility, apparently.

I stumbled around in the dark for a while, but eventually managed to get the power working again by manually restarting the magma generators(!) and reunited with Roland back at the Outpost, much to his relief.

The ‘Resistance’ guy gave me plans for making empty encrypted drives so that I can gather data from servers like the one I found in Rho, which I’ll be able to send to them via a new, jailbroken version of the Trade Node…

Once I’ve built that and sent over the first set of data, I should be able to take Roland back through the teleporter with me and take a look at what lies outside the Rho facility… Another underground jungle, perhaps?")

LW_PlayerLog_Theta_Discovered.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1742EB6142D30C11F122A4BBEFFFA2EA", "The Theta facility is a living nightmare.

The whole place is infested by some sort of living growth, maybe a fungus of some sort? Flesh-like masses grow on every surface, and the plant life around here is corrupted and disgusting.

Turns out this underground wonderland is not exclusively beautiful…

This disgusting stuff seems to be giving off a huge amount of spores, and I can’t get any further into the facility without some sort of protection against them. I dread to think what this stuff is doing to my lungs.

I need to find a vaccine or antigen, something I can use to ward off the spores.")

LW_PlayerLog_Theta_Explored.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "382F3EAF42BD2018D6381EB9102BA5A6", "I managed to grow a special form of this weird mycelial fungus which allowed me to create a temporary antigen against the spore infestation in Theta.

I got into the bunker within the facility (the one place where the infestation didn’t manage to get, thankfully) and retrieved the keycard of the Head Botanist here. If she’s the one responsible for this nightmare then I’m thankful she’s not around now to do any more damage.

Theta seems to have been this expedition's center for biological research (obvious now, in hindsight) and food production... What was a mining expedition even doing research like this for? So strange.

Another keycard down, anyway!")

OC_PlayerLog_CliffbaseHabitat_Discovered.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "94B890CC4834B38B24763CA028C39BD1", "There's another deserted habitat at the base of the cliffs.

It looks like the power is on inside, but the door's servos are fried... Of course.

If I can replace the burnt-out servos in the door mechanism, I'll be able to get in there, replenish my air supply and have a new jumping-off point for exploring the area.")

OC_PlayerLog_ClifftopHabitat.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "30D6D263479BB4C465A35A8A9031F71B", "I've made it past the cave-in, and entered the abandoned habitat. This place is nothing like the wrecks and scrap I've passed before now, this is very different.

It must be a remnant of the previous expedition, but the power is on, the hab is generating fresh air and everything is in good condition... How is this possible after so long?

Whatever's going on, this looks like a good place to rest up and return to when I'm out exploring.

The old expedition seems like it was a pretty significant operation, so there are sure to be some supplies around that I can use - once I get the Trade Node up and running and gain access to the market, I can even sell them!")

OC_PlayerLog_ClifftopHabitat_ElevatorDis.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "AA904D204984A2EEE8C4EAB03B67F7E6", "The habitat I found in the outer caverns is at the top of a large cliff, overlooking a vast cavern. 

There's also an elevator that connects it to the plateau below, and I can see what looks to be a huge meteor crater off in the distance.

The elevator appears to be out of action - it's missing a power source, and won't be taking me anywhere until I manage to find one.

If I can get it working, it'll make it a lot quicker to get back and forth deeper into the cavern.

There must be plans for a power source around here somewhere... If I can't find one, maybe I can buy some plans from ARC via the Trade Node?

Once I have the plans, it's just a matter of sourcing the materials, crafting it, and slotting it into the elevator.

I'll have this mechanical beauty purring again in no time, and my legs will certainly thank me!

That crater in the distance could be crammed with valuable resources and new ores, so that’s my next destination.")

OC_PlayerLog_CraterFacility_Bodies_Disco.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "8CC32A004D9FBDD3E108B8BEF61B8469", "Ok, what the hell is going on...

We found two bodies - still in atmo-suits - at the comms tower near the crater facility.

There's no obvious cause of death, but I should be careful - perhaps this place wasn't exactly abandoned on purpose...

I found a keycard on one of the bodies that I can use to release the lockdown in the Delta facility. If we can access the climber, we can go deeper and find out what happened here.

We’ve found records on facilities named ‘Beta’ and ‘Delta’ so far - is there a ‘Kappa’ somewhere, too? Whatever this ‘module’ is that Grandad was rambling about would be there, surely? Definitely need to go deeper.")

OC_PlayerLog_CraterFacility_Climber_Bloc.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "B82C5AE24EA64F241B367BB4A474ACAC", "I should have known it wasn't going to be simple. 

Releasing the lockdown got us into the climber room, but a blast door is covering the shaft and we can't get through.

Every time I think we're going to find out what's going on here, a new obstacle rears its head.... I just wanted to start something of my own here! I didn't want all of this complication!

Ugh. Enough moaning. We'll head back to the Outpost and think up a solution to getting through this blast door.")

OC_PlayerLog_CraterFacility_Discovered.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "310432E94EC4F8C3E01AF1AB525BB4E3", "I made it to the meteorite crater… There’s an enormous facility nestled within it, that I couldn’t see from the clifftop! It’s extensive, and filled with a bunch of supplies! 

At first glance, this appears to be a storage facility, but Roland has accessed the internal systems and discovered that this place - 'Facility Delta', apparently - is actually the uppermost station of something called the 'Scarpa Climber'...

Roland seems to think that it's an elevator, burrowing into the crust of the planet, with other facilities like this one at deeper points underground.

This whole operation is much larger than I’d expect for an expedition of the size that was apparently here before - what’s going on?

There's still no-one to be found around here, it really does appear that all of this has been abandoned... Why anyone would construct all of this and then leave is beyond me.

We're going to attempt to find a way to release the facility's lockdown and see if we can find out any more about what's going on - I found some messages between facility personnel about a comms tower nearby, I should check there first.")

OC_PlayerLog_CraterFacility_Solution_Fou.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "1B3D477D4A4823096315139E95F38F15", "Looks like I’m going to be making an EMP charge to blow open this blast door.

Not literally. Roland thinks we can fry the door and get it open with an electromagnetic pulse, and he’s a super intelligent AI construct, so who am I to second guess him?

Who am I kidding, I definitely do, but this time he really seems to have come up with a good idea!")

OC_PlayerLog_EMP_Crafted.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "25B30C084A73C541ACB481889BDAA1E8", "I’ve made the EMP charge, now to plant it on the blast door within the crater facility and retreat to a safe distance…

I won't be ringing up Trudy to let her know what we're up to though! Can’t take the chance of ARC swooping in and ruining everything.

Speaking of Trudy, what are the chances that she would end up being my ARC rep on this expedition? There must be dozens of managers at her level, but I somehow ended up with the one that I worked for before?...

She's actually been surprisingly nice so far, but I doubt she's doing it out of the goodness of her heart. ARC have only one goal - make as much money as possible - and I guess they're waiting to see if I can help them do that.")

OC_PlayerLog_EMP_Detonated.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "6B0E55DC4640C841E158F7AE2EC8FF7F", "The EMP charge was detonated safely from the surface with no catastrophic immediate effects, so we’re already doing better than a lot of ARC-run projects I’ve had the misfortune to be part of. 

This whole thing still seems really weird, but I just know that what we find down there will make this whole expedition worthwhile. I feel it in my bones.

Time to head back down and find out what awaits us in that climber shaft… Maybe I'll grab some new upgrades beforehand?")

OC_PlayerLog_Epsilon_PreIntro.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "4C2341BA43A246FB453DB4870D856962", "We made it down to the next climber station.

After detonating the EMP, I got a really strange message over the comms channel…

It was hard to understand what they were saying, they didn’t make a lot of sense… Could they be a survivor of this place? Someone from a neighboring claim? We’ll keep an eye out.

We’re now in another large facility, who-knows-how-deep into the planet. I’m going to take a look around and see what we’re working with here, but the amount of potential salvage here is astronomical.

Doesn’t look like I can get any further down on the climber - the system mentions a ‘Facility Omicron’ but it seems like there’s some sort of issue further down, and it won’t take me there.

This new contact over the radio is strange, and obviously we’ll try and help anyone we can if they’re still down here, but all being well we’re looking at a big payday once we process all this salvage! Cha-ching!")

OC_PlayerLog_HeadUnderground.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "0348BFCB4DDDDF60209D3A989A064CB9", "I've constructed the beginnings of my outpost here, though there's not much to be said for it yet.

I know that human discovery has always advanced by standing on the shoulders of giants, but I do wish I'd been able to prepare a little more, instead of needing to cannibalise the scrap of those who came before...

Oh well. No room for ego now. I have shelter to protect me from the dust storms, that's the important thing.

Time to head underground and see what we're working with here. Roland says he's detecting energy readings which might be useful for expanding the outpost, so I'll check that out first.

Maybe more shoulders to stand on?")

OC_PlayerLog_IntroToColony.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "05A75B0745DDD1EC15A935936AE63715", "I've finally arrived at the claim!

I have to admit, my initial impression is... underwhelming.

The rusting remains of the previous expedition here lie all around me; they're long gone now, and I know they apparently didn't have much success, but with all of Grandad's stories about this claim and some sort of 'Kappa Module', there has to be a reason he'd want me to come here.

That said, I can't help but feel apprehensive.

I've risked everything in this venture - if I can't make this expedition a success, I'm going to end up slinking back to ARC with my tail between my legs and begging for my old job back...

This has to pay off.")

OC_PlayerLog_OuterCavernsRockfall.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "42BE75D743EA5305ABD7219FA8F942EB", "The passage deeper into the underground has been blocked by a large cave-in, but I can see an old ARC-standard habitat behind the rockfall! An opportunity for some easy salvage?

Without access to the ARC network, I can't purchase the upgrade needed to power up Roland's laser (his ‘fangs’ as he likes to call them…) and breach this rockfall... Never mind that I don't have any credits left to buy it anyway!

Roland himself figured out a solution, fortunately. I knew I kept him around for a reason!

I'll need to...
- Mine resources in the cavern.
- Construct or find Facilities that can refine these resources into others.
- Construct a Crafting Bench in the Outpost.
- Craft Mining Explosives at the Crafting Bench using my newly refined resources.
- Blast through the rockfall by throwing the Mining Explosives - I can use my suit's Scan ability to determine what rocks are breakable.

I may need to make more than one set of explosives to create a usable passage.

Time to get to it!")

OC_PlayerLog_OuterCavernsSetLoose.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D4E707F843CD70EF0CCBDC9C8DAFAF93", "The outpost is now up and running with all basic functionality required for the mining operation to gather pace!

I've re-established a connection to the ARC Network, so I can now buy construction blueprints and crafting plans from the corporation, as well as sell them anything I find during my expeditions.

Apparently ARC have some sort of promotional scheme running that they're calling the 'Resource Acquisition Partner Program' or 'RAPP' - yuck. It's probably another useless marketing gimmick but it looks like I’ll be able to unlock new stuff as I level up by spending credits in the store (of course…), and maybe there are actually some useful rewards for participating.

Either way, Roland told me that gemstones are of particular interest to ARC and would fetch a good price, so if I can sell a few then I should have the credits to start buying stuff. They’re always crammed in amongst everything else in the resource nodes I keep finding, so I just need to keep mining!

I should continue exploring deeper into the caverns in search of more valuable resources, and attempt to reach that distant crater I can see from the clifftop.

In order to venture further, I can always purchase upgrades for my suit to improve its air supply, and plans I can use to craft items to assist me - all at the Trade Node. Handy!")
